title: Add CSV download/export for Milk Production Tracker

requirements.summary: Add CSV export functionality to the Milk Production page that downloads all currently displayed (filtered) milk production records
acceptanceCriteria: A 'Download CSV' button is visible on the MilkProduction page, near the records table or filter controls.
Clicking the button triggers a browser file download of a .csv file.
The CSV file contains a header row and one row per milk production record currently shown in the table.
Exported columns include at minimum: Date, Cattle ID or Name, Milk Quantity (Liters).
If filters are applied (e.g., date range, cattle selection), only the filtered records are exported.
The exported filename is descriptive, e.g., milk-production-YYYY-MM-DD.csv.
The button is disabled or shows a message when there are no records to export.

file_operations.description: Add a new exportMilkProductionRecordsToCSV function that accepts an array of MilkRecord objects (with cattle data joined) and generates a CSV file with columns for Date, Cattle ID, Cattle Breed/Name (if available), Quantity (Liters), and Notes. Follow the existing pattern used in the current CSV export utility, formatting the date appropriately and handling the nanosecond-to-Date conversion. The function should trigger a browser download with a filename like 'milk-production-YYYY-MM-DD.csv'.

Add a 'Download CSV' button near the table or filter controls that calls the new exportMilkProductionRecordsToCSV function. The button should be disabled when filteredRecords is empty. Enhance the filtered records data by joining with cattle information (breed/ID) from the getAllCattle query before passing to the CSV export function, ensuring the exported CSV includes readable cattle identifiers. Position the button in the UI near the existing filter controls for easy discoverability.
